00:18:48	grapebaba:	Hi, I'm transcribing this call with my Tactiq AI Extension. https://tactiq.io/r/transcribing
00:19:48	Gajinder Singh:	https://hackmd.io/@qYrlZEprQ1iz7NjiiEmDNQ/HytzBiWmzx
00:23:58	T. Wambsgans:	but by default (when there are no divergent heads), you don't need Type2 right ?
00:24:29	alan xu:	If proof aggregation is already an issue on devnet, will it become an even greater issue on mainnet, where the number of validators is significantly larger?
00:25:04	Anshal:	Replying to "but by default (wh..."

no we always need type2 because we fold the block signature as well in block proof, so even without any divergent heads and sync network there are atleast 2 messages
00:27:09	Gajinder Singh:	Replying to "If proof aggregation..."

type2 aggregation is just limited to max 8 payloads doesn't matter number of validators
00:27:24	Anshal:	Replying to "If proof aggregati..."

we will have more committees so should not get linearly worse atleast. Plus the current aggregator nodes are also not very buffed and ofc we are not considering eventual improvements that we get on aggregation side and client improvements
00:27:37	Gajinder Singh:	Replying to "If proof aggregation..."

type1 recursive aggregation in second stage by aggregators will make it feasible  to handle a huge number of validators
00:28:20	Gajinder Singh:	Replying to "If proof aggregation..."

this is whyy two stage layeer aggregation removes the complexity/compute away from the proposers/normal validators to the specialized aggregators
00:28:32	T. Wambsgans:	Replying to "but by default (when..."

Not sure to understand. You mean you also aggregate the XMSS coming from block producer to sign its block, with the rest of the signatures (from attesters) ?
